subject: The EP-WXT trigger 01709251266 is likely a flaring star
body:
B.T. Wang (YNAO, CAS),	 Q. J. Huang, R. Shi (PMO, CAS), H.Z. Wu (HUST), K. R. Ni (CCNU) and H. Sun (NAO,CAS) on behalf of the Einstein Probe (EP) team:

The EP-WXT trigger 01709251266 at the time of 2026-01-12T18:09:57, is likely a stellar flare associated with LSPM J0924+3041. The estimated flux of the flare is around 6x10^(-11) erg/s/cm^2 in 0.5-4.0 keV, corresponding to an X-ray luminosity of around 7.3x10^30 erg/s.

Launched on January 9, 2024, EP is a space X-ray observatory to monitor the soft X-ray sky with X-ray follow-up capability (Yuan et al. 2022, Handbook of X-ray and Gamma-ray Astrophysics).
